THOUGHT: The crash occurs in the SLP vectorizer when it builds a vector cast and the TargetFolder tries to constant-fold it into an invalid ConstantExpr cast. The culprit is the MinBitWidth-based opcode adjustment inside the Cast handling, which currently only excludes floating-point types but may also trigger for pointer casts (PtrToInt/IntToPtr). This can incorrectly switch the opcode to Trunc/ZExt/SExt for pointer-related casts, leading to an invalid constantexpr cast. I will restrict the MinBitWidth-based opcode adjustments to pure integer element types only by checking both source and destination scalar element types are integers, and introduce a DstScalarTy variable to simplify the check. Then I'll submit the patch.

```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
import re, sys
p = 'llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/SLPVectorizer.cpp'
s = open(p, 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()

# 1) Insert DstScalarTy after SrcScalarTy definition in the cast vectorization case.
s_new = re.sub(
    r'(Type \*SrcScalarTy = cast<VectorType>\(InVec->getType\(\)\)->getElementType\(\);\s*)',
    r'\1      Type *DstScalarTy = ScalarTy->getScalarType();\n',
    s,
    count=1
)

# 2) Limit MinBW-based opcode adjustments to integer element types only (exclude pointers).
s_new = re.sub(
    r'if \(\s*!ScalarTy->isFPOrFPVectorTy\(\)\s*&&\s*!SrcScalarTy->isFPOrFPVectorTy\(\)\s*&&',
    r'if (DstScalarTy->isIntegerTy() && SrcScalarTy->isIntegerTy() &&',
    s_new,
    count=1
)

if s_new == s:
    print("No changes applied; pattern not found.", file=sys.stderr)
else:
    open(p, 'w', encoding='utf-8').write(s_new)
    print("Patched:", p)
PY
&& submit-patch
```

I will fix this by replacing any DstScalarTy->isIntegerTy() checks with ScalarTy->getScalarType()->isIntegerTy(), ensuring the integer-only guard applies without needing a new variable. Then I will run submit-patch to build and validate the fix.

```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
import re, subprocess, sys, pathlib

p = pathlib.Path('llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/SLPVectorizer.cpp')
s = p.read_text(encoding='utf-8')

changed = False

# Replace any stray uses of DstScalarTy integer checks introduced earlier.
new_s = re.sub(r'DstScalarTy->isIntegerTy\(\)', 'ScalarTy->getScalarType()->isIntegerTy()', s)
if new_s != s:
    s = new_s
    changed = True

# Also handle the full condition pattern if it was inserted verbatim.
new_s = s.replace('if (DstScalarTy->isIntegerTy() && SrcScalarTy->isIntegerTy() &&',
                  'if (ScalarTy->getScalarType()->isIntegerTy() && SrcScalarTy->isIntegerTy() &&')
if new_s != s:
    s = new_s
    changed = True

# Ensure we did not accidentally leave any DstScalarTy declaration behind.
new_s = s.replace('      Type *DstScalarTy = ScalarTy->getScalarType();\n', '')
if new_s != s:
    s = new_s
    changed = True

if changed:
    p.write_text(s, encoding='utf-8')
    print("Replaced DstScalarTy integer checks and cleaned declarations.")
else:
    print("No DstScalarTy references found to replace; nothing changed.")

# Build and run validation.
res = subprocess.run(["submit-patch"], text=True)
sys.exit(res.returncode)
PY
```

I will make the cast creation robust by validating the opcode with CastInst::castIsValid and, if invalid, falling back to a valid opcode computed by CastInst::getCastOpcode. I will fix both cast creation sites: (1) the main cast vectorization block around line ~13474 and (2) the alternating-opcode cast pair around lines ~14048/14050. import pathlib, re, subprocess, sys, textwrap, tempfile, os

p = pathlib.Path('llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/SLPVectorizer.cpp')
s = p.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
orig = s

# 1) Make CreateCast robust in the main cast vectorization block (around ~13474).
pattern1 = re.compile(
    r'Value \*V = \(VecOpcode != ShuffleOrOp && VecOpcode == Instruction::BitCast\)\s*\?\s*InVec\s*:\s*Builder\.CreateCast\(VecOpcode,\s*InVec,\s*VecTy\);'
)

    "Value *V = (VecOpcode != ShuffleOrOp && VecOpcode == Instruction::BitCast)\n"

    "                            ? Builder.CreateCast(VecOpcode, InVec, VecTy)\n"
    "                            : Builder.CreateCast(\n"
    "                                  CastInst::getCastOpcode(InVec, /*SrcIsSigned*/ false,\n"
    "                                                          VecTy, /*DstIsSigned*/ false),\n"
